angle: Market-dominance: PLAT-005 framed as a Caples Why question
hook_type: Caples Why + the registered dominance number (more than 64%)
tactic: Why-dominance question hook -> answer (eight years as the venue) -> it is opening its own offer -> waitlist
primary_text:
Why has more than 64% of Australian crowd-sourced funding gone one way?

For eight years one platform has been where Australians back the companies they believe in. That same platform is now opening its own crowd-sourced funding offer.

Now you can own a piece of it. You read the offer document first, the day it goes live.

Leave your email and we will tell you the moment the offer goes live.

*Always consider the general CSF risk warning and offer document before investing.
headline: Why most CSF money goes one way
description: Why more than 64% of CSF went one way. Leave your email.
_source_label: [64% CITATION PENDING] DRC V5c -- DOM2 Market-dominance / PLAT-005 Why-question (first-timers)
_iterates: BIRCHAL-V5-W set (waitlist regime). New V5c structure: 4 angles x 4 hook-distinct variants under learnings-meta-ad.md #5 + Generation prompt addendum (one idea per sentence, ONE number then a turn, reader is subject at the turn and CTA, literal email micro-action CTA per WAITLIST-CTA 2026-05-18).
_v5c_rationale: One of 4 hook-distinct variants in its angle; the hook (first 1-2 sentences) is the primary differentiator, body adapted to fit the hook (JB-permitted 2026-05-18).

Built to the learnings-meta-ad.md #5 / Generation prompt addendum 8 structural gates: opens with ONE verified number (or Caples Why on a verified number) as a complete sentence, full stop, then a turn; the reader is the grammatical subject at the turn and the CTA; no third-party company names in the bridge; plain concrete nouns; one proof point per beat; CTA = literal email micro-action with the exchange; verbatim CSF warning byte-exact terminator.

CTA field is the WAITLIST-CTA approved verbatim 'Save my spot' (landing-page canonical button); the in-copy close uses the 2026-05-18 JB-preferred literal email micro-action 'Leave your email and we will tell you the moment the offer goes live' (FMTH top-performer pattern, CSF-clean notification framing).
Strict WAITLIST regime: Birchal's own CSF offer is not live until 26 June 2026 (OFFER-CONTEXT). No transaction verbs, no $ minimum, no preferential-terms framing. Pre-offer compliant.
_drc_playbook: 03 Aspirational Identity Sell (B2C considered, solution-aware, sophistication 3 mechanism)
_drc_audience: B2C considered, solution-aware. First-time CSF investors; the Why opens the dominance fact as curiosity.
_a_pile_signals: specific number in first 8 words (more than 64%)
_compliance_findings_resolved: PLAT-005 usage: used ONCE, registered scoped wording 'more than 64% of Australian crowd-sourced funding', 'CSF in Australia' scope, never with #1/first/largest. Paired with verified spine (eight years). source_status OPEN: DRAFT/JB-review only, not marked live until citation attached.
PREOFFER-VERBS: 'Leave your email', 'we will tell you the moment', 'read the offer document first'. No transaction verbs.
VERBATIM-OFFER-LANGUAGE: 'we will tell you the moment the offer goes live'. No 'we will send'.
VERBATIM-CSF-WARNING: byte-exact final line, appears exactly once.
No OTHER market-share / #1 / largest / leading / first / valuation. Single share figure only, not stacked.
Subject discipline: 'Birchal' zero times; 'one platform' / 'that same platform' resolved with first-person 'its own crowd-sourced funding offer'.
Number traceability: more than 64%=PLAT-005 (citation-pending), eight years=PLAT-004.
